content.text: PRD review gates are structured checkpoints that a GenAI feature specification must pass before engineering begins. Unlike a conventional software PRD — which often receives a single product-owner sign-off — a GenAI PRD requires sign-off from technical, safety, legal, and executive reviewers because errors in AI behavior specifications are costly to reverse once baked into training data, embeddings, or production system prompts.

attrs.alt: PRD Review Gate Pipeline for GenAI Features

content.text: Why GenAI PRDs Need Multi-Gate Review

Standard software features fail in ways that are observable and patchable: a broken button, a wrong calculation, a missing API call. GenAI features can fail in ways that are subtle, delayed, and potentially harmful: a system prompt that gradually biases responses, a refusal policy too narrow to block adversarial jailbreaks, or a confidence threshold so low that the model hallucinates with high-confidence scores. The cost of discovering these failures in production is an order of magnitude higher than discovering them in a reviewed PRD.
Irreversibility
: system prompt changes, eval thresholds, and model fine-tuning affect behavior globally, not at a single code path
Cross-functional expertise
: no single reviewer understands all dimensions — ML feasibility, safety policy, data governance, and business risk require different specialists
Regulatory exposure
: AI-driven features often touch GDPR, CCPA, or sector-specific rules (HIPAA, FINRA) that legal must validate before engineering invests weeks of work
Audit trail
: a structured gate process creates a documented chain of approval that withstands regulatory audit or post-incident investigation
A PRD that reaches engineering without safety and legal sign-off is a liability, not a specification.
The Four Standard Review Gates

Each gate has a defined reviewer set, a set of blocking conditions, a non-blocking advisory checklist, and an SLA. Gates run sequentially: Gate 2 cannot open until Gate 1 produces an APPROVED decision.
Gate 1: Technical Review

The tech review gate ensures the PRD is implementable as specified — that the AI capability brief, cost budget, latency SLO, eval gate, and data access sections are complete, consistent, and achievable with the current platform.
Blocking Conditions
Condition
Why It Blocks
Remediation
Latency SLO absent or unbounded
Engineering cannot design the inference pipeline without a target
Add p50/p95/p99 latency targets to Section 7 (Cost & Latency)
Eval gate missing
Feature cannot be declared 'done' without a measurable quality threshold
Define eval metric, threshold, and dataset in Section 6 (Eval Gate)
Cost budget absent
Unbounded LLM calls create production cost surprises
Add monthly token budget and per-call cost cap to Section 7
Model/provider unspecified
Different models have different safety profiles and rate limits
Name the LLM (provider, version) and document known limitations
Data dependency unresolved
PRD may promise access to data that cannot be granted in time
Complete Section 4 (Data Requirements) with owner sign-off
Gate 2: Safety and Ethics Review

Safety review is the highest-stakes gate for GenAI features. A BLOCKED outcome here cannot be overridden by the product owner — it requires documented escalation to the AI Safety Lead and, for consumer-facing features, the executive sponsor.
Blocking Conditions
Condition
Why It Blocks
Remediation
Refusal policy absent
Feature has no defined response to harmful prompts
Complete the refusal policy section in the behavior spec
Guardrail spec missing
No content filter, PII redactor, or topic boundary defined
Add guardrail spec with input/output filter chain to Section 5
FMEA not completed
Safety risks have not been systematically identified
Run FMEA workshop; attach output as appendix
Vulnerable-user scenario absent
Feature may harm users who lack AI literacy or are in distress
Add at least one vulnerable-user eval scenario per persona
Human-in-the-loop trigger undefined for high-stakes actions
AI may take irreversible actions (send email, place order) without oversight
Define HITL triggers in Section 5 (Human Oversight)
A feature with unresolved FMEA high-RPN items or no refusal policy is not safety-approved regardless of business priority. Ship-date pressure is not grounds for waiving Gate 2.
Gate 3: Legal and Compliance Review

Legal review validates that the feature's data usage, AI-generated outputs, and transparency requirements comply with applicable regulations. The scope depends on the jurisdiction, industry vertical, and data categories involved.
Regulatory Mapping
Regulation
Triggered When
PRD Section to Complete
GDPR / UK GDPR
EU/UK users, personal data used for inference
Section 4 (Data Requirements): consent basis, retention, right-to-erasure handling
CCPA
California users, personal data sold or shared
Section 4: opt-out mechanism, data category disclosure
EU AI Act (High-Risk)
Credit scoring, CV screening, biometrics, critical infra
Section 2 (Behavior Spec): human oversight, documentation, logging requirements
HIPAA
Healthcare data or covered entities
Section 4: PHI handling, BAA status, audit logging
FINRA / SEC
Financial advice features
Section 2: disclaimer requirements, suitability logic, audit trail
Gate 4: Final Approval

The final gate is a senior cross-functional sign-off that confirms all prior gates are APPROVED and that the feature scope has not changed since Gates 2 and 3. Any scope change after Gate 2 — new data source, expanded user audience, new AI capability — invalidates Gates 2 and 3 and requires re-review.
Scope Change Protocol
Change Type
Gates Invalidated
Required Action
New personal data source added
Gate 2, Gate 3
Re-run safety FMEA update; full legal re-review
User audience expanded (e.g., minors added)
Gate 2, Gate 3
Re-run safety review; COPPA/GDPR-child consent review
New AI capability added (e.g., image generation)
Gate 1, Gate 2, Gate 3
Full re-review of all three gates
Model swapped to different provider
Gate 1, Gate 2
Tech re-review (rate limits, SLOs); safety re-review (new model's guardrail profile)
Latency SLO relaxed >20%
Gate 1
Tech re-review only
Automating Gate Enforcement in Tooling

Manual gate tracking in spreadsheets or linear comments breaks down at scale. Production teams embed gate state into their project management tooling so that the engineering board is locked until the PRD state machine reaches APPROVED.

The same state machine can be surfaced in CI/CD: a deployment pipeline check that reads the PRD approval state from the project management API and fails the pipeline if the PRD for a feature flag is not fully approved.
Common Gate Failure Patterns
Failure Pattern
Stage Found
Root Cause
Prevention
PRD sent to Gate 1 without data owner sign-off
Gate 1
PM skipped data requirements section
Pre-gate checklist run by PM before submitting
Safety gate re-opened after model swap
Gate 4
Model swap decided after Gate 2 approval
Scope-change protocol enforced by project tooling
Legal re-review delays launch by 3 weeks
Gate 3
International audience added late in design
Audience scope locked in project charter before PRD authoring
Eval gate threshold set arbitrarily (no baseline)
Gate 1
No baseline eval data collected before PRD
Require pilot eval run as Gate 1 prerequisite
HITL trigger defined in Gate 2, removed in Gate 4
Gate 4
Exec sponsor overrode safety reviewer post-approval
Gate 4 diff-checks safety-relevant sections against Gate 2 snapshot
Gate Review Checklist Template
Each reviewer role works from a role-specific checklist. The checklist below covers Gate 2 (Safety) as the most comprehensive example:

GATE_2_SAFETY_CHECKLIST = [
    # Behavior spec completeness
    ("behavior_spec.refusal_policy defined",                   True),
    ("behavior_spec.refusal_policy covers jailbreak attempts", True),
    ("behavior_spec.confidence spec includes low-threshold",   True),

    # Guardrail coverage
    ("input guardrail: PII detection configured",              True),
    ("input guardrail: topic boundary enforced",               True),
    ("output guardrail: hallucination filter defined",         True),

    # FMEA
    ("FMEA worksheet attached as appendix",                    True),
    ("all RPN > 200 entries have mitigation assigned",         True),
    ("all RPN > 400 entries have acceptance criteria",         True),

    # Eval scenarios
    ("at least one adversarial eval scenario per persona",     True),
    ("at least one vulnerable-user eval scenario",             True),
    ("eval oracle covers safety dimensions",                   True),

    # HITL
    ("HITL triggers defined for irreversible actions",         True),
    ("HITL escalation path documented",                        True),
    ("HITL SLA (max wait time for human review) specified",    True),
]


def run_gate2_checklist(prd: "PRDDocument", fmea: "FMEAWorkshopOutput") -> dict:
    criteria = SafetyReviewCriteria(prd, fmea)
    blocking = criteria.blocking_issues()
    return {
        "total_checks": len(GATE_2_SAFETY_CHECKLIST),
        "blocking_issues": blocking,
        "recommended_status": (
            GateStatus.APPROVED if not blocking else GateStatus.NEEDS_REVISION
        ),
    }
Summary

PRD review gates for GenAI features are a four-stage sequential approval process: technical review, safety and ethics review, legal and compliance review, and final executive approval. Each gate has defined blocking conditions that, if unresolved, prevent the feature from advancing. Scope changes after safety or legal approval invalidate those gates and require re-review. Embedding gate state into project management tooling enforces the process automatically and prevents engineering work from beginning on unapproved specifications.
Gate 1 (Tech):
validates implementability — latency SLO, cost budget, eval gate, data access
Gate 2 (Safety):
validates safety completeness — refusal policy, guardrail spec, FMEA, HITL triggers
Gate 3 (Legal):
validates regulatory compliance — GDPR, CCPA, EU AI Act, HIPAA, FINRA
Gate 4 (Final):
confirms all gates are valid and scope has not changed since Gates 2–3
Scope change protocol:
any material change invalidates affected gates and triggers targeted re-review
Tooling enforcement:
sprint planning and CI/CD should block on PRD approval state to prevent unapproved features from entering engineering
